ucs.title: [LLM] Cl0p / FIN11 MOVEit Transfer LEMURLOOT web-shell post-exploitation — w3wp.exe (MOVEitDMZ AppPool) spawning recon/exec utilities
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Cl0p/FIN11 (Spandex Tempest / GRACEFUL SPIDER) MOVEit Transfer compromise chain: SQLi against the public MOVEit Transfer endpoint drops the LEMURLOOT web shell (human2.aspx / human2.aspx.cs) which is executed by the MoveItDMZAppPool IIS worker (w3wp.exe). Any child process other than MOVEit's own helpers spawning from w3wp.exe on a MOVEit host is high-fidelity exploitation evidence — this exact pattern is how Cl0p enumerated and exfiltrated data from ~2,700 victims in the May–June 2023 campaign and is still the canonical Cl0p MFT tradecraft (re-used against GoAnywhere MFT a
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as command_lines values(Processes.process_path) as child_paths values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=w3wp.exe Processes.process_name IN (cmd.exe,powershell.exe,pwsh.exe,net.exe,net1.exe,whoami.exe,systeminfo.exe,ipconfig.exe,nltest.exe,quser.exe,tasklist.exe,reg.exe,wmic.exe,bitsadmin.exe,certutil.exe,rundll32.exe,mshta.exe,wscript.exe,cscript.exe,curl.exe,sc.exe,arp.exe,route.exe,ping.exe) by host Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_path Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process Processes.user 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` 
| `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` 
| join host type=left [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name=human2.aspx OR Filesystem.file_name=human2.aspx.cs OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\MOVEitTransfer\\wwwroot\\*") by host Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name | rename Filesystem.file_path as suspicious_file_path Filesystem.file_name as suspicious_file_name | fields host suspicious_file_path suspicious_file_name] 
| where isnotnull(suspicious_file_path) OR count>0
ucs.kql:
// Cl0p / FIN11 — MOVEit LEMURLOOT post-exploit: IIS worker for MoveItDMZAppPool spawning non-MOVEit binaries
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","reg.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","rundll32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","curl.exe","sc.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","ping.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe"]);
let Spawns = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MoveItDMZAppPool","MOVEitDMZ","MOVEitTransfer","MOVEit Transfer")
         or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\MOVEitTransfer\"
    | where FileName in~ (SuspiciousChildren);
let ShellDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where (FileName =~ "human2.aspx" or FileName =~ "human2.aspx.cs")
         or (FolderPath has @"\MOVEitTransfer\wwwroot\" and FileName endswith ".aspx"
            and FileName !in~ ("human.aspx","guestaccess.aspx","login.aspx","DMZLogin.aspx","machine.aspx"))
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, ShellFile = FileName, ShellPath = FolderPath,
              DropperProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine, SHA256;
union isfuzzy=true Spawns, ShellDrops
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ChildProcess = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ShellFile, ShellPath, DropperProcess, DropperCmd, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cl0p / FIN11 — MOVEit LEMURLOOT post-exploit (SecurityEvent 4688 + W3CIISLog)
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic([@"\cmd.exe",@"\powershell.exe",@"\pwsh.exe",@"\net.exe",@"\net1.exe",@"\whoami.exe",@"\systeminfo.exe",@"\ipconfig.exe",@"\nltest.exe",@"\quser.exe",@"\tasklist.exe",@"\reg.exe",@"\wmic.exe",@"\bitsadmin.exe",@"\certutil.exe",@"\rundll32.exe",@"\mshta.exe",@"\wscript.exe",@"\cscript.exe",@"\curl.exe",@"\sc.exe",@"\7z.exe",@"\7za.exe"]);
let Spawns = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName endswith @"\w3wp.exe"
    | where NewProcessName has_any (SuspiciousChildren)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
              ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, Source="ProcessCreate";
let ShellHits = W3CIISLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where csUriStem has_any ("human2.aspx","/human2/")
         or (csUriStem endswith ".aspx" and csUriQuery has_any ("X-siLock-Comment","X-siLock-Step1","X-siLock-Step2","X-siLock-Step3"))
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, cIP, csUserAgent, csUriStem, csUriQuery, scStatus,
              Source="IIS";
union isfuzzy=true Spawns, ShellHits
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Cl0p (FIN11) MOVEit LEMURLOOT Web Shell Post-Exploitation - IIS Worker Spawning Recon/Exec Utilities
id: 7b2e0f1a-cl0p-moveit-w3wp-childproc
status: stable
description: Detects MoveItDMZAppPool IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) spawning command interpreters or discovery utilities — the canonical Cl0p/FIN11 post-exploitation pattern after LEMURLOOT web shell deployment against CVE-2023-34362.
references:
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/zero-day-moveit-data-theft
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/06/07/clop-ransomware-gang-exploits-moveit-vulnerability/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0092/
author: detection-eng
date: 2026/05/13
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0092
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_parent_context:
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - 'MoveItDMZAppPool'
      - 'MOVEitDMZ'
      - 'MOVEitTransfer'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\7za.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and (selection_parent_context or selection_child) and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate MOVEit Transfer scheduled maintenance scripts (rare and from documented paths)
level: high
ucs.rationale: MOVEit Transfer is a niche, narrowly-scoped server product — its IIS worker (w3wp.exe under MoveItDMZAppPool) should ONLY ever spawn a tiny set of MOVEit-internal child processes (DMZService, IIS housekeeping). cmd/powershell/recon-LOLBins as a child is essentially never legitimate. This *exact* child-of-w3wp pattern is documented across the FBI/CISA AA23-158A advisory, Mandiant's TR-23-MOVEit rep
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Cl0p Cleo MFT (Harmony/VLTrader/LexiCom) autorun-XML exploit chain — javaw.exe spawning PowerShell/cmd from Cleo install path
ucs.description: Hunts Cl0p's October–December 2024 mass-exploitation campaign against Cleo Harmony, VLTrader, and LexiCom managed-file-transfer servers (CVE-2024-50623 / CVE-2024-55956 — autorun-directory file-write → arbitrary command execution). The Cleo server runs as javaw.exe out of its install path; an attacker drops a malicious XML/shell payload into the `autorun\` subdirectory which the engine executes, spawning PowerShell or cmd as a child of javaw.exe. Cl0p publicly claimed these victims on its DLS, and Huntress / Sophos / Rapid7 corroborated this exact javaw→PS chain as the post-exploit primitive.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as child_path values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name=javaw.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=java.exe) (Processes.parent_process_path="*\\Cleo\\Harmony\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\Cleo\\VLTrader\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\Cleo\\LexiCom\\*") Processes.process_name IN (powershell.exe,pwsh.exe,cmd.exe,wmic.exe,certutil.exe,bitsadmin.exe,rundll32.exe,mshta.exe,wscript.exe,cscript.exe,net.exe,whoami.exe,nltest.exe,7z.exe,7za.exe,curl.exe) by host Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_path Processes.process_name Processes.process 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` 
| `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` 
| join host type=outer [| tstats summariesonly=true count as autorun_drops values(Filesystem.file_name) as autorun_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Cleo\\Harmony\\autorun\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Cleo\\VLTrader\\autorun\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Cleo\\LexiCom\\autorun\\*") by host]
ucs.kql:
// Cl0p — Cleo Harmony/VLTrader/LexiCom CVE-2024-50623/CVE-2024-55956 autorun exploit chain
let CleoPaths = dynamic([@"\Cleo\Harmony\", @"\Cleo\VLTrader\", @"\Cleo\LexiCom\"]);
let CleoAutorun = dynamic([@"\Cleo\Harmony\autorun\", @"\Cleo\VLTrader\autorun\", @"\Cleo\LexiCom\autorun\"]);
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wmic.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","rundll32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","curl.exe"]);
let JavaSpawns = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("javaw.exe","java.exe")
    | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (CleoPaths)
    | where FileName in~ (SuspiciousChildren);
let AutorunDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where FolderPath has_any (CleoAutorun)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("javaw.exe","java.exe","explorer.exe")
         or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("-encodedcommand","FromBase64String","DownloadString","WebRequest","curl ","Invoke-Expression","IEX(")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AutorunFile = FileName, AutorunPath = FolderPath,
              Dropper = InitiatingProcessFileName, DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              SHA256, FileSize;
union isfuzzy=true 
    (JavaSpawns | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ChildProcess = FileName,
        ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine, ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
        ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine, Source = "JavaChild"),
    (AutorunDrops | extend Source = "AutorunDrop")
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cl0p — Cleo Harmony/VLTrader/LexiCom autorun exploit chain (Sentinel — SecurityEvent 4688)
let CleoPaths = dynamic([@"\Cleo\Harmony\", @"\Cleo\VLTrader\", @"\Cleo\LexiCom\"]);
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic([@"\powershell.exe",@"\pwsh.exe",@"\cmd.exe",@"\wmic.exe",@"\certutil.exe",@"\bitsadmin.exe",@"\rundll32.exe",@"\mshta.exe",@"\wscript.exe",@"\cscript.exe",@"\net.exe",@"\whoami.exe",@"\nltest.exe",@"\7z.exe",@"\curl.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith @"\javaw.exe" or ParentProcessName endswith @"\java.exe"
| where ParentProcessName has_any (CleoPaths)
| where NewProcessName has_any (SuspiciousChildren)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, LogonId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Cl0p Cleo MFT (Harmony/VLTrader/LexiCom) Autorun Exploit Chain - Java Process Spawning Interpreters
id: 9c41a2d3-cl0p-cleo-autorun-cve2024-50623
status: stable
description: Detects post-exploitation of CVE-2024-50623 / CVE-2024-55956 against Cleo Harmony, VLTrader, and LexiCom MFT servers by Cl0p ransomware. The Cleo service (javaw.exe) processes a planted autorun XML and spawns PowerShell or cmd as a child — characteristic Cl0p mass-MFT-compromise tradecraft (Oct–Dec 2024 campaign).
references:
  - https://www.huntress.com/blog/cleo-software-actively-being-exploited-in-the-wild
  - https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/2024/12/11/etr-cve-2024-50623-cleo-multiple-products-unrestricted-file-upload-and-download/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0092/
author: detection-eng
date: 2026/05/13
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0092
  - cve.2024.50623
  - cve.2024.55956
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent_image:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\javaw.exe'
      - '\java.exe'
  selection_parent_path:
    ParentImage|contains:
      - '\Cleo\Harmony\'
      - '\Cleo\VLTrader\'
      - '\Cleo\LexiCom\'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
  condition: selection_parent_image and selection_parent_path and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Documented Cleo administrative scripts invoked from the autorun directory (rare)
level: critical
ucs.rationale: Cleo MFT is a small-footprint enterprise product with a well-defined process tree — javaw.exe under the Cleo install path should not spawn Windows command interpreters under any normal operating condition. Cl0p is the only actor publicly attributed to mass-exploitation of CVE-2024-50623/CVE-2024-55956 (claimed ~66 victims on its leak site, corroborated by Huntress, Rapid7, Sophos, and Mandiant Dec
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
